# Protein Fitness Prediction Model using ESM2 backbone
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import esm

class ProteinFitnessModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, esm_model_name="esm2_t30_150M_UR50D", freeze_backbone=False):
        super().__init__()
        # Load ESM2 model
        self.esm_model, self.alphabet = esm.pretrained.load_model_and_alphabet_hub(esm_model_name)
        
        # Freeze backbone if specified
        if freeze_backbone:
            for param in self.esm_model.parameters():
                param.requires_grad = False
        
        # Get embedding dimension
        embed_dim = self.esm_model.embed_dim
        
        # Add regression head
        self.regression_head = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Dropout(0.1),
            nn.Linear(embed_dim, embed_dim // 2),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.1),
            nn.Linear(embed_dim // 2, embed_dim // 4),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Dropout(0.1),
            nn.Linear(embed_dim // 4, 1)
        )
        
        # Initialize final layer
        nn.init.zeros_(self.regression_head[-1].weight)
        nn.init.zeros_(self.regression_head[-1].bias)
    
    def forward(self, input_ids, attention_mask=None):
        # Get embeddings from ESM model
        results = self.esm_model(input_ids, repr_layers=[30], return_contacts=False)
        # Use the last layer representations
        embeddings = results["representations"][30]  # Shape: (batch, seq_len, embed_dim)
        
        # Average pooling over sequence length
        if attention_mask is not None:
            # Mask padded positions
            masked_embeddings = embeddings * attention_mask.unsqueeze(-1)
            sum_embeddings = masked_embeddings.sum(dim=1)  # Sum along sequence dim
            count = attention_mask.sum(dim=1).unsqueeze(-1)  # Count non-padded tokens
            pooled_embeddings = sum_embeddings / count.clamp(min=1)  # Average
        else:
            pooled_embeddings = embeddings.mean(dim=1)  # Average along sequence dim
        
        # Pass through regression head
        output = self.regression_head(pooled_embeddings)
        return output.squeeze(-1)  # Return scalar prediction for each sample
